entries.definition:
كتاب الخاء

خسق - خسل

(خسر) الخاء والسين والراء أصلٌ واحدٌ يدلُّ على النَّقْص. فمن ذلك الخُسْر والخُسْران، كالكُفْر والكُفْران، والفُرْق والفُرْقان. ويقال خَسَرْتُ المِيزانَ وأخْسَرْتُه، إذا نقَصْتَه. والله أعلم.

{باب الخاء والشين وما يثلثهما}

kh–s–r to go astray, to lose one’s way, to perish; to incur a loss, to lose, a loss; to decrease, to give less than what is right, to give short measure; to misguide. Of this root, 10 forms occur 65 times in the Qur’an: خَسِرَ khasira 16 times; يُخْسِرُون yukhsirūn twice; خُسْر khusr twice; خَاسِرُون khāsirūn 32 times; خَاسِرَة khāsiratun once; خَسَار khasār three times; خُسْرَان khusrān three times; أَخْسَرُون ʾakhsarūn four times; تَخْسِير takhsīr once and مُخْسِرُون mukhsirūn once.
خَسِرَ khasira a [v. trans.] 1 [with no obj.] to suffer a loss, to lose [as in a race] (10:45) قَدْ خَسِرَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِلِقَاءِ اللَّهِ those who denied the meeting with God have indeed lost 2 to lose something (7:9)

263

وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَازِينُهُ فَأُولٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ and those whose scales [good deeds] are light will be the ones who have lost their souls.

يُخْسِر yukhsir [imperf. of v. IV أَخْسَرَ ʾakhsara, trans.] to give less than the correct amount, to shortchange, to give short measure/weight (55:9) وَأَقِيمُوا الْوَزْنَ بِالْقِسْطِ وَلَا تُخْسِرُوا الْمِيزَانَ set up the balance with justice, and do not give short measure [lit. cause the balance to lose/be lost].

خُسْر khusr [v. n./n.] loss, failure, ruin (65:9) فَذَاقَتْ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهَا وَكَانَ عَاقِبَةُ أَمْرِهَا خُسْرًا and it tasted the ill effect of their conduct–the result of their conduct was ruin.

خَاسِرُون khāsirūn [pl. of act. part. خَاسِر khāsir, fem. خَاسِرَة khāsiratun] one who loses the right way, a loser, a sinner (10:95) وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ and do not be one of those who deny the revelations of God for then you will become one of the losers.

خَسَار khasār [v. n.] loss, losing (71:21) وَاتَّبَعُوا مَنْ لَمْ يَزِدْهُ مَالُهُ وَوَلَدُهُ إِلَّا خَسَارًا and followed those whose riches and children only increased their losses.

خُسْرَان khusrān [v. n.] loss, losing (4:119) وَمَنْ يَتَّخِذِ الشَّيْطَانَ وَلِيًّا مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ فَقَدْ خَسِرَ خُسْرَانًا مُبِينًا whoever takes Satan as a patron instead of God, has surely suffered a clear loss.

أَخْسَرُون ʾakhsarūn [pl. of elat. أَخْسَر ʾakhsar] most losing, greatest losers, farthest astray from the right path (18:103) قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِالْأَخْسَرِينَ أَعْمَالًا say ‘[Prophet] shall I tell you who lose most by their actions?’

تَخْسِير takhsīr [v. n.] loss, losing (11:63) فَمَا تَزِيدُونَنِي غَيْرَ تَخْسِيرٍ who could protect me from God if I disobeyed Him?, you would only make my loss greater [lit. you would not increase me other than in loss].

مُخْسِرُون mukhsirūn [pl. of act. part. مُخْسِر mukhsir] one who gives less than due, one who gives short measure, one who cheats in dealing with others (26:181) أَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ وَلَا تَكُونُوا مِنَ الْمُخْسِرِينَ give full measure: do not be among those who give others less than their due.

264
